blocks.content: When the library door closed, he couldn't hold back and burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hahaha! Shirone, you're the best! I've never seen my brother make that kind of expression before!
Even as Rian hugged Shirone like a doll and shook him, Shirone couldn't take his eyes off where Rai had left.
Swordsman versus Magician.
The single book Rian had brought raised many questions for Shirone. Perhaps, as Rai said, reality might be different.
But Shirone believed.
That the spirit of a magician who fearlessly puts even his weaknesses on the experimental table is the very reason intelligence exists in this world.
The grand library felt eerily empty.
The bookshelves once filled with knowledge were now vacant, and the glow lamps that used to illuminate the place like daylight were turned off.
A single beam of golden sunlight poured in from the fourth-floor window, developing on the floor.
Shirone turned the pages while basking in that sunlight.
One page, then another. And finally... the last page ended, and the cover closed.
Shirone caressed the back of the orange-red cover.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Flipping a book is an easy task. But making it through hundreds of pages of valleys to finally flip it over wasn't something just anyone could do.
The history of 850 volumes.
Shirone had read all the history books in the grand library.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It"s over. This is the backbone of my knowledge.
It felt warm. Like light was filling him up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone could now understand the world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Though he was a hunter's child, he could discuss general worldly matters with any noble without issue.
It was the result of selection and focus.
Talent merely suggests an efficient direction; anyone who reads 850 history books can realize where they stand.
The library relocation project had also been completed after a major undertaking of one year and six months.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He could go home anytime now, and no longer had to worry about his family dying.
It felt like a dream.
Numerous memories flashed through his mind like fragments of a dream.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hey, Shirone. You're still here? I thought the work was finished?
Shirone greeted his friend with a gentle smile.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What's with that expression? Did something good happen?
Rian was perplexed by his friend's unusual demeanor.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rian, show me around your house.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Huh? What did you say?
Rian's eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You... you did it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. There's nothing left to read, at least not in this library.
Nothing left to read.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Huh? What did you say?
Rian didn't like books, but he could feel how impressive that statement was.
In this moment, Shirone looked like the embodiment of intellect.
Even the sunlight pouring through the window seemed to bless him.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Whahaha! You really did it? Man! You're truly amazing!
Rian rejoiced as if it were his own achievement.
In his own aggressive way, of course.
After hugging Shirone tightly and swinging him around for a while, he snapped his fingers as if he'd had a good idea.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Alright! If you want to come to our house, you're always welcome!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: This is perfect timing. I've prepared an awesome gift to celebrate the end of your studies.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What gift all of a sudden? I don't need anything.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Just follow me, I tell you. You'll love it! Hahaha!
Shirone felt uneasy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was strange that Rian claimed to have prepared a gift when he'd just learned about the completion of the reading.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Moreover, the bulging laugh lines under his eyes suggested it definitely wouldn't be a gift Shirone would appreciate.
Seeing the mansion up close for the first time, its imposing size was overwhelming.
He understood why ancient people chose massive structures to represent the divinity of gods.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rian, let's go in quietly. What if your family catches us?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What are you worried about? The contract's over anyway, right? Besides, everyone in the family is out.
Ah, this way. Hurry, hurry!
The place Rian took him to was a woman's room with a fragrant scent.
Pink curtains hung over the balcony window, and various musical instruments, including a piano, were arranged around the room.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait! This is your sister's room, isn't it? Let's get out! What are we doing here?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hehehe. Just wait a bit. It should be somewhere around here.
Rian rummaged through the dresser drawers as if it were his own room.
Searching the bottom shelf, he grabbed something and threw it at Shirone with a triumphant cheer.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Found it! Here, Shirone, a gift!
Something fragrant and soft covered Shirone's face.
When he held it up with both hands, it was a tiny piece of cloth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey! Are you crazy? Why are you throwing this at me!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hahaha! Look at how red your face is. You're already 17. You should be able to look at panties boldly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shut up! What's bold about looking at panties! Take it back quickly!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Oh? You're excited. Shirone is finally excited!
Rian clutched his stomach and stomped his feet in laughter.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: In truth, nine-tenths of him just wanted to tease Shirone, but one-tenth was genuinely concerned.
Having been cooped up in the library for a year and a half, Shirone's skin was pale white, and his face still couldn't shed its childish appearance.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone was too hungry for knowledge.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It wouldn't be a bad idea to use shock therapy once in a while to bring out the man inside him.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: But it didn't work on Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: After returning the panties, Shirone sighed and gave his usual nagging.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: When will you ever grow up? Playing such pranks. It's your sister, of all people. Put it back quickly. What if we get caught?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: She's a court musician and only comes home once or twice a year. We won't get caught anyway. Are you sure you don't want to keep it? Don't blame me if you regret it later.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Following you is my life's biggest regret! Put it away quickly.
Rian crumpled the underwear and stuffed it back into the drawer.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hey, we'll get caught if you do it like that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The dedicated maid will organize it when she has time. Anyway, it's no fun since you're not excited.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I'll cut ties with you if you do this one more time.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hahaha! Alright. It was just a joke. Anyway, let's go to my room.
Rian's room was at the end of the corridor.
He said since most rooms were vacant anyway, he picked the one he found comfortable.
Befitting a passionate swordsman, wooden swords were stacked in his room, and the bookshelf contained books related to swordsmanship.
However, there weren't many professional books; most were novels about mythical swordsmen.
After sitting in the room and chatting about various things, the two played a card game when they got bored.
They both bet the same amount of chips, but the game ended in just 30 minutes.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No way! Damn it. Gambling is supposed to be about luck, but I guess I'm extremely unlucky.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You seem to have a habit of moving your cards outward when you get an ace. And the time you take to bet is different when you have a pair versus when you don't.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just knowing those two things, anyone could easily beat you.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: How do you find out such things?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Observation is surprisingly easy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You don't see because you're too fixated on preconceived notions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let go of the idea that you must find something, and feel the whole picture.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then you can spot it.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hmm, so don't obsess over what you want to find, right? This might help in finding the schema too, don't you think?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Probably? It had a big influence on my understanding of the spirit zone.
Rian repeated Shirone's words countless times.
His mind felt ticklish, as if he was close to understanding something, but he just couldn't grasp the core.
Perhaps from thinking hard, which was unusual for him, his body felt sluggish and drowsiness overwhelmed him.
Stretching widely, Rian lay down and said, 'There's nothing to do, so let's sleep for a bit.'

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I ran a hundred laps this morning too, so I'm dead tired.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Go ahead and sleep. I'll read a book.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Mm-hmm.
Rian fell asleep as soon as he put his head down.
Shirone chuckled at his carefree friend and picked up a novel.
After reading only difficult history books, time flew by as he immersed himself in adventure stories.
When Rian didn't wake up even after an hour, Shirone closed the book and tidied up the room.
It seemed best to leave now, as the house would become bustling when the sun set.
As he walked along the corridor, he heard piano music.
Shirone stopped in shock.
For someone raised in the mountains, sheet music inspired by a composer stirred his heart in a way different from magic.
The sound was coming from Rian's sister's room.
Moving his feet unknowingly, Shirone saw Rian's sister for the first time.
A pure face like the dawn dew.
A woman with blue hair flowing like waves was playing the piano.
Ozent Reina, the second daughter of the Ozent family.
The melody Reina played was truly heavenly.
Though not the youngest, she was recognized for her talent and entered the royal palace at the age of 19, so Shirone's assessment wasn't exaggerated.
The harmonies building up.
The rush of those harmonies.
And then variation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's mind followed Reina's fingertips like a marionette caught in the melody.
Only after the performance ended did Shirone realize where he was.
Perhaps the emotion lasted too long.
By the time he came to his senses, Reina had already turned around and was waving at him.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Hello? How was my piano sonata?
He had read about it in books, but this was his first time hearing it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You can come closer and look. I haven't seen your face before. Are you a new butler?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Ah! I've heard about you. You must be very smart. Not just anyone can do classification work.
Perhaps because she was an artist, her words and actions weren't authoritative at all; rather, they were considerate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. I was in charge of the library classification work. It's finished now, though.
For the first time, Shirone envied Rian for having a sister.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then he remembered the inappropriate crime he had unintentionally committed.
His cheeks flushed as her face overlapped with what he had seen.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh!

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Are you feeling unwell? Why does your face look like that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, no, it's nothing.
Shirone awkwardly entered the room.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Is this what they call sheet music?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm? Yes, that's right. It's Schraiman's Piano Sonata, 3rd Movement, 'In the Darkness.'

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Have you ever played the piano?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, never.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Really? Sit over here. I'll teach you. It's not a difficult piece.
Shirone took a liking to her kind and affectionate nature.
Of course, he was also bewildered, having heard from Rian that she was a witch with a nasty temper.
Shirone pressed the first chord, paying attention to the fingering as instructed.
Then he moved at intervals to connect to the second chord.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Wow! You have very quick eyes. For someone playing for the first time, your sight-reading is really good.
Sight-reading refers to playing a score for the first time.
It requires technical talent rather than sensitivity, and someone with excellent sight-reading can be considered a fast thinker.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Excuse me...

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Yes?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could you play it once more for me?
At that moment, Reina discovered Shirone's unique temperament.
The idea that artists like talent is a lie fabricated by critics.
Rather, artists love passion.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Alright. Listen carefully.
Reina encored 'In the Darkness.'
Even to an amateur like Shirone, it was a much more delicate performance than the first.
This was the power of the schema.
Her nervous system, which understood her own body, was twice as sensitive as an ordinary person's.
After the performance, Reina turned her head with a satisfied face.
But Shirone didn't express any reaction, as if he hadn't heard anything, and was lost in thought.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: May I try?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please understand if it's hard to listen to.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Hehe, of course. Everyone starts that way.
Shirone's hands gently rested on the keyboard.
Just as Reina thought his posture was decent, Shirone's fingers began to tap the keys.
As unfamiliar music played, Reina smiled awkwardly.
But as it progressed to the middle section, she seemed to realize something and leaned in to listen.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: What is this child? What is he trying to do?
All the notes were off. Completely wrong.
But amazingly...
It was perfectly similar.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Clearly, Shirone lacked the technical proficiency to play sheet music.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: But excluding the technical part, it was as if he had become Reina, imitating everything about her.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: This wasn't a performance for someone else.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: In this child's mind, there was only the piano.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'A little more. Just a little closer.'
Shirone was desperately trying to penetrate the music.
Maintaining the rhythm made the development too fast.
With his sight-reading skills, he couldn't hit the exact notes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He compressed his concentration, which fluctuated every second.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His state of selflessness began to be pulled into the spirit zone.
Reina swallowed hard.
Pleasant harmonies were occasionally interspersed among the discordant sounds.
And finally, during the climax that went 'du-du-du-du!' the performance matched the score perfectly for exactly 1.7 seconds.
Reina felt a thrill and held her breath.
Shirone maintained that state and entered the authentic cadence.
After striking the tonic, he raised both hands and forcefully hit the dominant.
Then he pressed the final tonic with the greatest passion of his life.
Kuuuuuuung.
The harmonies that had continued from four minutes in the past dissipated into seven notes, spreading infinite reverberations.
